items.title: Xiaomi 开源 MiMo-V2.5-Pro (1.02T MoE, 强 Agent 编码)
items.summary: 4 月 28 日小米开源 MiMo-V2.5 系列(MIT)。Pro 版 1.02T/42B 激活,混合注意力(128-token 滑窗 : 全局 = 6:1,KV cache ~7×↓),1M 上下文。配 MiMo Code agent harness,厂商基准在 SWE-bench Verified(82 vs 79)/Pro(62 vs 55)/Terminal-Bench 2(73 vs 69)超 Claude Code+Sonnet 4.6。尚无 Ascend 移植。数字均厂商口径,provisional。

items.title: Seed 2.1 Pro 抢先版上线 LMArena (闭源, 编码/Agent)
items.summary: 6 月 19 日 ByteDance 把 Seed-2.1-Pro-Preview 放上 Code Arena 试用:前端编码子榜 1539 Elo、全球第 8,约等于 Claude Opus 4.6。闭源 API、无 Ascend 移植——是标杆而非 NPU 载荷。完整基准随正式版未来几周公布。分析见 Blog → Dispatch 01。

items.title: 新一批开源模型 (6月中): Qwen3 Coder Next · MiniMax M2.7 · Nemotron 3 Ultra
items.summary: 6 月中又一波开放权重发布:Qwen3 Coder Next、MiniMax M2.5/M2.7 Highspeed、NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra 等(新闻聚合,基准/细节待官方)。

items.title: DeepSeek-V4 & GLM-5 run on Ascend 910B (vLLM-Ascend)
items.summary: vLLM-Ascend's 2026 support matrix adds DeepSeek-V4 (custom 910B ops, MTP KV-cache sharding for spec decoding) and GLM-5 (W8A8C8 quant); Qwen3.5 + W8A8 across Qwen3/DeepSeek/MiniMax. MiniMax-M3 not yet listed.

items.title: GLM-5.2 open-sourced (MIT, 1M context)
items.summary: Launched Jun 13 2026; MIT open weights now live on Hugging Face (zai-org/GLM-5.2). 753B params (GLM-5.1-class 744B MoE / 40B active), context 200K→1M, max output 131K, coding-first. No official benchmarks at launch; media cites it edging GPT-5.5 on long-horizon coding (FrontierSWE/SWE-Marathon) at ~1/6 cost — treat as provisional.
items.ascendNote: GLM-5 is supported on vLLM-Ascend (W8A8C8 quant); GLM-5.2 not yet listed by name.

items.title: MiniMax-M3 with MSA sparse attention
items.summary: Open-weight 1M-context model, native multimodal + agentic coding. MSA (blockwise sparse on GQA) cuts attention compute ~28.4x at 1M context. Official report on arXiv.
items.ascendNote: MiniMax family has W8A8/QuaRot on vLLM-Ascend (M2.x); M3 not yet listed by name.

items.title: DeepSeek-V4 (1.6T MoE, 1M context)
items.summary: V4-Pro/Flash with hybrid sparse attention (CSA+HCA), Multi-Token Prediction, and a multi-teacher distillation pipeline over 32T+ tokens. Official tech report PDF on Hugging Face.
items.ascendNote: Supported on Ascend 910B via vLLM-Ascend (custom ops, MTP KV-cache sharding for speculative decoding).

items.title: Agentic RL: a distinct paradigm (survey)
items.summary: Survey reframing agentic RL vs single-turn RLVR — multi-turn credit assignment, tool/environment interaction, long-horizon planning.

items.title: Stable Asynchrony: variance-controlled off-policy RL
items.summary: Controls heavy-tailed importance weights from stale rollouts to keep async/off-policy GRPO stable at high asynchrony.

items.title: Ascend 950PR / 950DT announced (FP8, self-built HBM)
items.summary: Next-gen Ascend with native FP8/MXFP4 and Huawei's own HBM — 950PR (inference) ~Q1 2026, 950DT (training) ~Q4 2026. Opens the door to FP8 RL on Ascend.

items.title: vLLM-Ascend v0.13.0 released
items.summary: Latest Ascend rollout backend — broader model coverage and performance; the rollout engine RL stacks target on NPU.

items.title: DeepSeek-V3.2 + Sparse Attention (DSA)
items.summary: Open-weight frontier model with trainable sparse attention for cheaper long-context; RL post-training continues the R1 lineage.

items.title: GDRO-RL: distributionally robust GRPO
items.summary: Rollout-/Prompt-GDRO reweight toward worst-case prompt distributions on top of GRPO for more robust reasoning RL.

items.title: MindSpeed-RL: Distributed RL Post-Training on Ascend NPU
items.summary: Native distributed GRPO-family RL on Ascend, with experiments up to DeepSeek-R1-MoE-671B on a 384-NPU superpod — the strongest large-scale RL-on-Ascend evidence to date.

items.title: AsyncFlow: Asynchronous Streaming RL Post-Training
items.summary: An async streaming RL framework targeting the throughput limits of synchronous/colocated RL — directly relevant to NPU constraints like the missing vLLM sleep mode.

items.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Pure large-scale RL (GRPO + verifiable rewards) elicits emergent reasoning with no SFT (R1-Zero); catalyzed the 2025 RLVR wave.

items.title: GSPO: Group Sequence Policy Optimization
items.summary: Sequence-level importance ratios and clipping fix GRPO instability and notably stabilize MoE RL; contributed to Qwen3.

items.title: DAPO: An Open-Source LLM RL System at Scale
items.summary: GRPO + Clip-Higher, dynamic sampling, token-level loss, and overlong reward shaping; 50 on AIME 2024 with Qwen2.5-32B at half the steps.

items.title: ProRL: Prolonged RL Expands Reasoning Boundaries
items.summary: Stabilizes very long RL runs (KL control, reference resetting) and shows prolonged RL uncovers genuinely new reasoning strategies.

items.title: MiniMax-M1: Test-Time Compute with Lightning Attention (CISPO)
items.summary: Open hybrid-attention reasoner trained with CISPO (clips IS-weights, not tokens); full RL on 512 H800s in ~3 weeks.

items.title: AReaL: Large-Scale Asynchronous RL for Language Reasoning
items.summary: Fully decouples rollout from training with staleness-enhanced PPO for ~2.77x speedup; now ships a stable Ascend branch.

items.title: Native Sparse Attention (NSA)
items.summary: Trainable, hardware-aligned hierarchical sparse attention matching full attention with large speedups at 64K length.

items.title: The Entropy Mechanism of RL for Reasoning LLMs
items.summary: Explains policy-entropy collapse via log-prob/advantage covariance and proposes Clip-Cov / KL-Cov to sustain exploration.

items.title: Pretraining LLMs with NVFP4
items.summary: Large-scale 4-bit (NVFP4) pretraining of a 12B model on 10T tokens matching FP8 — the most ambitious FP4 training run reported.

items.title: GLM-4.5: Agentic, Reasoning, and Coding Foundation Models
items.summary: Hybrid thinking/direct MoE (355B/32B) trained with a difficulty-based RL curriculum on the open-source 'slime' framework.

items.title: Multimodal Reward Hacking in Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) is increasingly used to align multimodal large language models (MLLMs), but higher rewards do not always imply better task performance. This risk is amplified when visual evidence is evaluated by text-only or weakly grounded rewards. We study reward hacking in MLLM RL acr…

items.title: Bidirectional Resource Scheduling for Disaggregated and Asynchronous RL Post-Training
items.summary: It is well established that the reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs) can be improved by applying reinforcement learning (RL) in a post-training stage. In a standard RL iteration, the current model (the policy) generates experience through rollouts, and the resulting data is then us…

items.title: Mach-Mind-4-Flash Technical Report
items.summary: We present Mach-Mind-4-Flash, a 35B-parameter Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) agentic model with 3B activated parameters. Through post-training optimization alone without scaling pre-training compute, the model achieves performance on par with or surpassing that of 100B-parameter-class models. By introduci…

items.title: A Sovereign, Open-Source Foundation Model for German and English
items.summary: We present Soofi S 30B-A3B, a sovereign, open-source Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) hybrid Mamba Transformer foundation model for German and English. Its hybrid design activates only 3B of 30B parameters per token and keeps the inference cache near-constant as context grows, giving it a decisive throughpu…

items.title: Self-Guided Test-Time Training for Long-Context LLMs
items.summary: Long-context processing has become increasingly important for large language models (LLMs), but simply extending the context window does not guarantee effective utilization of long inputs. As input length grows, accuracy often degrades, indicating that models still struggle to identify and use the e…

items.title: General Non-Clairvoyant KV-Cache Scheduling via Regime-Aware Routing
items.summary: We study non-clairvoyant scheduling for batched Large Language Model (LLM) inference under a hard Key-Value (KV) cache memory budget. Each request has a known prompt length but an unknown response length, and its memory footprint comprises a fixed prompt component together with a response component …

items.title: KV-PRM: Efficient Process Reward Modeling via KV-Cache Transfer for Multi-Agent Test-Time Scaling
items.summary: Process Reward Models (PRMs) have been proven to be highly effective in guiding test-time scaling (TTS) methods, which significantly boost the capabilities of LLM-based multi-agent systems. However, existing PRMs are text-based: they re-encode the entire trajectory text from scratch. In long multi-a…

items.title: COBS: Cumulant Order Block Sparse Attention
items.summary: Block sparse attention is a hardware friendly way to alleviate the key-value (KV) cache read bottleneck in large language models (LLMs). However, it is not prevalent among leading open-weight LLMs, which rely instead on dense attention or fine-grained selection, thereby motivating our analysis. We s…

items.title: Do You Need a Frontier Model as a Citation Verifier? Benchmarking Rubric LLMs for Deep-Research Source Attribution
items.summary: Reinforcement learning increasingly relies on an LLM judge to score each rubric criterion, and that judge acts as the reward model during training. Before such a signal can be trusted, we need to know how capable the judge must be and how biased it is. We study this calibration question for citation…

items.title: When Synthetic Speech Is All You Have: Better Call GRPO
items.summary: LLM-based ASR adapted to regulated domains such as banking is bottlenecked by privacy: real speech is costly and legally constrained to collect, making synthetic text-to-speech (TTS) an attractive substitute. Yet synthetic speech stays acoustically mismatched with real recordings, and work on this g…

items.title: DrugGen 2: A disease-aware language model for enhancing drug discovery
items.summary: Current computational approaches for drug design typically focus on generating molecules conditioned on specific targets or general molecular properties, often neglecting the influence of disease context on target behavior and therapeutic outcomes. To address this gap, we introduce DrugGen-2, a nove…

items.title: Compete Then Collaborate: Frontier AI Teachers Build a Verifiable Curriculum to Improve a Coding Student Beyond Imitation
items.summary: Large language models increasingly serve as teachers generating training data for smaller students. Prior multi-teacher knowledge distillation methods merge outputs without determining which frontier model teaches best, often relying on an LLM judge biased toward its own outputs. We introduce a comp…

items.title: MORES: Mobile Reasoning-as-a-Service via Distributed LLM Inference-Time Scaling
items.summary: Inference-time scaling has emerged as an effective approach for enhancing the capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs), addressing the growing demand for stronger reasoning without increasing model size. This novel form of LLM scaling comprises two representative approaches: explicit reasoning, …

items.title: Latent Memory Palace: Reasoning for Control as Autoregressive Variational Inference
items.summary: Human decision-making is highly flexible -- some actions are taken immediately; others require longer deliberation. Language models have exhibited a similar capacity for adaptive "reasoning." However, transferring this capability to continuous control policies has been challenging, as directly reaso…

items.title: Open-ended Multi-agent Autocurricula via Visual Inspection of Policies with Multi-modal LLMs
items.summary: Open-ended curricula in Reinforcement Learning (RL) aim to train generally-capable agents by identifying tasks that facilitate learning increasingly complex skills. A major challenge when designing such curricula is assessing task difficulty relative to the agent's current learning progress. While p…

items.title: ASMR: Agentic Schema Generation for Ship Maintenance Report Writing
items.summary: In this paper, we study the automatic schema generation problem: given a collection of historical ship maintenance and operational reports across multiple form categories, automatically discover compact and informative schemas that capture the essential information requirements of each report type. …

items.title: On the Limitations of Non-GPU AI Accelerators for Large-Model Inference: A Field Study of MoE and Multimodal Serving on Huawei Ascend
items.summary: Non-GPU AI accelerators are increasingly adopted as alternatives to general-purpose GPUs for large-model inference, but the real engineering cost of migrating demanding workloads beyond CUDA remains poorly documented. We present a field study of deploying two large inference workloads on a 16-device…

items.title: Understanding Axes of Difficulty For Long Context Tasks Via PredicateLongBench
items.summary: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated rapidly improving long-context capabilities, prompting a wave of benchmarks designed to evaluate them. However, existing long-context evaluations - from Needle-in-a-Haystack (NIAH) tests to more recent multi-hop reasoning and summarization tasks - predo…

items.title: Towards Efficient Large Language Model Serving: A Survey on System-Aware KV Cache Optimization
items.summary: Despite the rapid advancements of large language models (LLMs), LLM serving systems remain memory-intensive and costly. The key-value (KV) cache, which stores KV tensors during autoregressive decoding, is crucial for enabling low-latency, high-throughput LLM inference serving. In this survey, we foc…

items.title: BiSCo-LLM: Lookup-Free Binary Spherical Coding for Extreme Low-Bit Large Language Model Compression
items.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly constrained by memory capacity, weight bandwidth, and checkpoint storage during deployment. Existing low-bit compression methods mainly follow two directions. Scalar or group-wise quantization is simple and compatible with efficient low-precision kernels…

items.title: StreamDQ: Near-Memory Weight DeQuantization in Custom HBM for Scalable AI Inference Acceleration
items.summary: As large language models (LLMs) scale, their memory and computation demands have grown substantially, making weight-only quantization a widely adopted technique for reducing model size with minimal accuracy loss. However, on current GPUs, CUDA-core-based dequantization introduces substantial instruc…

items.title: It Takes a MAESTRO To Prune Bad Experts
items.summary: Sparsely-activated Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models achieve remarkable inference efficiency by activating only a small fraction of parameters per token, yet their full expert banks reside in memory at all times, creating a prohibitive deployment bottleneck. Existing structured pruning method…

items.title: When LLMs Agree, Are They Right? Auditing Self-Consistency and Cross-Model Agreement as Confidence Signals
items.summary: LLM-as-judge (Zheng et al., 2023) is increasingly the default for evaluating AI systems in enterprise pipelines, often scaled to ensembles (Verga et al., 2024) or "mixture-of-experts" (Shazeer et al., 2017) panels of judges. These systems share a key assumption: that consistency -- agreement among j…

items.title: Rethinking Small VLM Quantization: From Component-Wise Analysis to Hardware-Aware Edge Deployment
items.summary: The emergence of vision language models with fewer than 3 billion parameters has accelerated the implementation of on-device multimodal intelligence. However, a detailed understanding of component-wise quantization remains a bottleneck for optimal deployment. This paper presents a systematic evaluat…

items.title: Hallucination Self-Play: Bootstrapping Reinforced Detector via Evolved Generator
items.summary: Identifying faithfulness hallucinations in LLM-generated outputs remains challenging due to the scarcity of high-quality annotated data. Recent work relies on advanced LLMs to synthesize training data, including rationales, labels, and hallucinated claims. However, these methods treat the generator …

items.title: When Implausible Tokens Get Reinforced: Tail-Aware Credit Calibration for LLM Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has achieved remarkable success in enhancing the reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs). However, widely used critic-free RL methods rely on uniform credit assignment, broadcasting the same advantage to all tokens regardless of their differences. We identi…

items.title: When Does Continual Learning Require Learning
items.summary: As large language models (LLMs) become increasingly capable, the next question is how can we enable models to continually learn? Today, the field largely frames this as a problem of context management and mitigating forgetting. We argue this framing is incomplete: continual learning is fundamentally…

items.title: Selective Timestep Weighting and Advantage-Based Replay for Sample-Efficient Diffusion RLHF
items.summary: Reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for aligning generative models with human preferences. However, applying RLHF to diffusion models remains highly feedback inefficient, as existing approaches typically require large amounts of human or reward model …

items.title: Agon: Competitive Cross-Model RL with Implicit Rival Grading of Reasoning
items.summary: Reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (e.g. GRPO) is the engine behind today's reasoning models, yet it grades only the final answer. On hard problems this trains models to write more rather than to think better, since the trace itself is never graded and no label for good thinking exists. …

items.title: RubriQ: Rubric-Guided Group Relative Policy Optimization for Constraint-Aware Quantum Circuit Synthesis
items.summary: Designing fault-tolerant quantum circuits that are both algorithmically correct and hardware compatible remains a major bottleneck in the transition to scalable quantum computing. We introduce RubriQ, a scalable framework that formulates circuit synthesis as a large language model (LLM) code-generat…

items.title: Single-Rollout Asynchronous Optimization for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) is becoming increasingly important for post-training large language models (LLMs). Previous RL pipelines for LLMs were mostly synchronous and batch-interleaved, which is inefficient for long-horizon agentic tasks. Recently, asynchronous RL has emerged as a more efficient …

items.title: Selective Left-Shift: Turning Test-Time Compute and Difficulty-based Curation into Training Data for Low-Resource Code Generation
items.summary: Large Language Models achieve strong code generation for high resource languages like Python and Java but suffer sharp performance drops on Low-Resource Programming Languages~(LRPLs) such as Julia. Improving Small Language Models~(SLMs) for these languages faces a trilemma: Supervised Fine-Tuning~(S…

items.title: BUS: Brain-Inspired Unsupervised Self-Reflection via Backward Prediction for Multimodal Reasoning
items.summary: Current Vision-Language Models (VLMs) often struggle to handle complex visual tasks that require consistent and fine-grained reasoning. Recent methods aim to train models to facilitate self-reflective reasoning, i.e., reviewing and improving the generated reasoning. However, they require large volum…

items.title: UP: Unbounded Positive Asymmetric Optimization for Breaking the Exploration-Stability Dilemma
items.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become the standard paradigm for enhancing the complex reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs). To achieve sample efficiency, modern RL frameworks rely on importance sampling (IS). However, these algorithms suffer from an exploration-stability dilemma. …

items.title: The Key to Going Linear: Analysis-Driven Transformer Linearization
items.summary: The quadratic cost of causal self-attention severely bottlenecks long-context transformer inference. While numerous post hoc linearization pipelines exist, it is difficult to identify which components preserve model quality. This work isolates the effect of state update design in a strict frozen-bac…

items.title: Sparse Delta Memory: Scaling the State of Linear RNNs through Sparsity
items.summary: Linear attention models allow a fixed state size and a fixed amount of compute per token. However, due to their limited state size, linear attention models fall behind in long-context recall compared to softmax-attention-based transformer architectures. Increasing the state size of linear attention …

items.title: Jet-Long: Efficient Long-Context Extension with Dynamic Bifocal RoPE
items.summary: Modern LLMs are increasingly deployed in long-context applications such as retrieval-augmented generation, repository-level coding, and agentic workflows whose accumulated reasoning and tool traces routinely push the input an order of magnitude past the pretraining window, making zero-shot context e…

items.title: GIFT: Geometry-Informed Low-precision Gradient Communication for LLM Pretraining
items.summary: Gradient communication is a primary scaling bottleneck in large language model (LLM) pretraining. Communicating gradients in low-precision formats, such as FP8 and NVFP4, can significantly reduce the communication volume. Existing methods quantize gradients via linear or nonlinear mappings in Euclid…

items.title: KronQ: LLM Quantization via Kronecker-Factored Hessian
items.summary: Post-training quantization (PTQ) is a widely adopted technique for compressing large language models (LLMs) without retraining. Existing second-order PTQ methods, including GPTQ, construct quantization objectives exclusively from input activation statistics, effectively assuming that all output chan…

items.title: When Does In-Context Search Help? A Sampling-Complexity Theory of Reflection-Driven Reasoning
items.summary: Training large language models (LLMs) with extended reasoning has enabled in-context search, in which models iteratively generate, critique, and revise solution attempts. We provide a theoretical analysis of in-context search by modeling it as approximate inference over reasoning traces, where the b…

items.title: DepthWeave-KV: Token-Adaptive Cross-Layer Residual Factorization for Long-Context KV Cache Compression
items.summary: Long-context language model inference is increasingly limited by the memory bandwidth and capacity required to store key-value caches, yet existing compression methods often apply uniform budgets across layers or tokens and degrade retrieval when lexical cues and semantic states require different pr…

items.title: FreqDepthKV: Frequency-Guided Depth Sharing for Robust KV Cache Compression in Long-Context LLM Inference
items.summary: Long-context LLM inference is increasingly limited by the memory and bandwidth cost of KV caches, yet aggressive compression can remove the layer-specific evidence needed for retrieval and multi-step reasoning. We introduce FreqDepthKV, an inference-time cache compression method that factorizes adja…

items.title: LongCrafter: Towards Diverse Long-Context Understanding via Evidence-Graph-Guided Instruction Synthesis
items.summary: Synthesizing long-context supervised fine-tuning (SFT) data is a scalable way to enhance the long-context understanding of large language models (LLMs), yet existing approaches share three limitations: narrow task coverage, insufficient instruction difficulty, and a lack of faithfulness supervision.…

items.title: Causal ASCEND: Scalable Two-tier Causal Discovery on High Dimensional Multi-omics Data
items.summary: Biological systems exhibit a hierarchical structure, characterised by directed flow from upstream regulators to downstream effects. Although this ordering provides a natural scaffold for causal inference, most causal discovery and GRN methods either ignore the tiered organisation or condition on all…

items.title: HiFA4: Training-Free 4-bit FlashAttention on Ascend HIF4 NPUs for LLM Inference
items.summary: We present HiFA4, a post-training operator-level design that executes both QK^T and PV in FlashAttention as 4-bit HIF4 Cube GEMMs for LLM inference on Ascend NPUs, while maintaining the online softmax state in FP16. To our knowledge, HiFA4 is the first Ascend-HIF4-targeted design of this kind evalua…

items.title: Full-Stack FP4: Stable LLM Pretraining with Quantized Projections, Optimizers, and Attention
items.summary: Recent NVFP4 pretraining methods mainly target transformer linear layers, leaving optimizer states, optimizer arithmetic and attention underexplored in 4-bit pipelines. This critical gap blocks stable full-stack 4-bit pretraining, as the three core modules exhibit unique numerical failure patterns: …

items.title: MxGLUT: A Reconfigurable LUT-Centric Broadcast Dataflow Accelerator for Mixed-Precision GEMM
items.summary: Large language model (LLM) inference suffers from growing inefficiency across the prefill and decode phases, especially under weight-only quantization, where activations remain in FP8 while weights are compressed to low-bit integers. Existing LUT-based accelerators mainly target FP8-INT4 computation…

items.title: W4A4 Quantization for Inference on Wan2.2-I2V-A14B
items.summary: We summarize our submission to Sub-Challenge 1: W4A4 Quantization for Inference (HiF4 / MXFP4) of the ICME 2026 Low-Bit-width Large-Model Quantization Challenge. The sub-challenge targets 4-bit weight and 4-bit activation inference on Wan-AI/Wan2.2-I2V-A14B under HiF4 or MXFP4 numerical formats. We …

items.title: SharQ: Bridging Activation Sparsity and FP4 Quantization for LLM Inference
items.summary: Low-bit floating-point formats and semi-structured sparsity are increasingly supported by modern accelerators, yet combining them for LLM activation compression remains challenging: activations contain input-dependent outliers that dominate block scales in FP4 quantization, and directly applying N:M…
